keyPoints: A bipartite graph has two disjoint sets of vertices, U and V, with no edges between them.
In a bipartite graph, the incidence of an edge is either from U to V or from V to U.
The degree of a vertex in a bipartite graph is the number of edges from the set it belongs to.
An isolated vertex in a bipartite graph is a vertex with no edges connecting it to either set U or V.
